instagram_subtitle: Behind the scenes and daily inspiration.
about_bio_p1: Hi, I'm Alex — a professional photographer with over 12 years of experience capturing life's most meaningful moments. Based in the Pacific Northwest, I travel worldwide for weddings, portraits, and commercial assignments.
about_bio_p2: My approach blends documentary-style authenticity with fine-art composition. I believe every photograph should tell a story — one that resonates emotionally and stands the test of time.
about_bio_p3: When I'm not behind the camera, you'll find me hiking mountain trails, experimenting with film photography, or curating my ever-growing collection of vintage lenses.
about_philosophy_1: Light is everything. I chase the golden hour and embrace shadows.
about_philosophy_2: Connection before composition. Real moments come from trust.
about_philosophy_3: Less is more. I deliver curated galleries, not thousand-image dumps.
about_philosophy_4: Every client is unique. I tailor every session to your vision.
about_gear_body_1: Sony A7R V — 61MP of pure detail
about_gear_lens_1: Sony 24-70mm f/2.8 GM II — My everyday lens
about_gear_lens_2: Sony 85mm f/1.4 GM — The portrait king
about_gear_lens_3: Sony 70-200mm f/2.8 GM II — Ceremony essential
blogs_subtitle: Tips, techniques, and stories from the field.
blog_1_title: 10 Tips for Stunning Golden Hour Portraits
blog_1_excerpt: Master the art of golden hour photography with these essential tips for warm, glowing portraits.
blog_1_intro: The golden hour — that magical window shortly after sunrise or before sunset — is every photographer's dream. Here are 10 tips to make the most of it.

blog_1_sections.title: Plan Ahead
blog_1_sections.body: Use apps like PhotoPills or Sun Surveyor to know exactly when golden hour starts and ends at your location.

blog_1_sections.title: Shoot Wide Open
blog_1_sections.body: Golden hour light is soft and flattering. Use wide apertures (f/1.4–f/2.8) for dreamy bokeh and subject separation.

blog_1_sections.title: Backlight Your Subject
blog_1_sections.body: Position your subject between you and the sun for stunning rim light and ethereal glow.

blog_1_sections.title: Use a Reflector
blog_1_sections.body: Bounce warm light back onto your subject's face to fill shadows without losing the golden tone.

blog_1_sections.title: Experiment with Silhouettes
blog_1_sections.body: Expose for the sky and let your subject go dark for dramatic, artistic silhouettes.

blog_1_sections.title: Mind Your White Balance
blog_1_sections.body: Set white balance to 'Daylight' or 5500K to preserve the warm, golden tones naturally.

blog_1_sections.title: Scout Locations Early
blog_1_sections.body: Arrive 30–45 minutes before golden hour to find the best angles and compositions.

blog_1_sections.title: Shoot in RAW
blog_1_sections.body: RAW files give you maximum flexibility to recover highlights and lift shadows in post.

blog_1_sections.title: Use Lens Flare Creatively
blog_1_sections.body: Don't fear lens flare — use it as a creative element for dreamy, atmospheric images.

blog_1_sections.title: Work Quickly
blog_1_sections.body: Golden hour only lasts about 30–40 minutes. Have your settings dialed in and shoot efficiently.

blog_2_title: How to Prepare for Your Wedding Photography Session
blog_2_excerpt: A comprehensive guide to ensure your wedding photos are everything you've dreamed of.
blog_2_intro: Your wedding day is one of the most photographed days of your life. Here's how to prepare for stunning results.

blog_2_sections.title: Create a Shot List
blog_2_sections.body: Work with your photographer to list all must-have shots — family groupings, detail shots, and special moments.

blog_2_sections.title: Schedule a First Look
blog_2_sections.body: A first look session before the ceremony gives you intimate, emotional photos without the time pressure.

blog_2_sections.title: Plan for Light
blog_2_sections.body: Share your ceremony and reception timeline so your photographer can plan for changing light conditions.

blog_2_sections.title: Designate a Photo Wrangler
blog_2_sections.body: Assign a family member or friend who knows everyone to help gather groups for formal portraits.

blog_2_sections.title: Trust Your Photographer
blog_2_sections.body: You hired a professional for a reason. Trust their creative vision and enjoy your day.

blog_3_title: The Art of Black and White Photography
blog_3_excerpt: Why stripping away color can create more powerful, timeless images.
blog_3_intro: Black and white photography strips away distraction, leaving only light, shadow, texture, and emotion.

blog_3_sections.title: Why Black and White?
blog_3_sections.body: Without color, the viewer focuses on composition, contrast, and the emotional core of the image. It's timeless and powerful.

blog_3_sections.title: What Makes a Great B&W Photo
blog_3_sections.body: Strong contrast, interesting textures, leading lines, and compelling expressions all shine in monochrome.

blog_3_sections.title: Shoot with Intention
blog_3_sections.body: Don't just convert color photos as an afterthought. Learn to see in black and white — look for light, shadow, and form.

blog_3_sections.title: Post-Processing Tips
blog_3_sections.body: Use gradient maps and dodge/burn techniques rather than simple desaturation for rich, nuanced black and white conversions.

contact_success: Message sent successfully! I'll get back to you within 24 hours.
faqs_subtitle: Everything you need to know before booking.
faq_a1: Wedding packages start at $3,500, portrait sessions at $450, and commercial projects are quoted individually based on scope. Every package includes high-resolution edited images and a private online gallery. Contact me for a detailed pricing guide tailored to your needs.
faq_q2: How long is the turnaround time?
faq_a2: Wedding galleries are delivered within 4–6 weeks, portrait sessions within 2 weeks, and commercial projects per the agreed timeline. Rush delivery is available for an additional fee.
faq_a3: Absolutely! I love destination weddings and shoots. Travel within 100 miles of Portland is included. For destinations beyond, travel and accommodation costs are added to the package. I've shot in over 15 countries and am always passport-ready.
faq_q4: How many photos do we receive?
faq_a4: Weddings typically deliver 500–800 edited images. Portrait sessions deliver 50–100 images. Commercial projects vary by scope. I focus on quality over quantity — every image you receive is professionally edited.
faq_a5: My style blends documentary/photojournalistic authenticity with fine-art editorial aesthetics. I capture real moments with an artistic eye — think natural poses, beautiful light, and genuine emotion rather than stiff, overly posed shots.
faq_a6: I don't provide RAW files as standard, as editing is an integral part of my artistic process. However, RAW files can be purchased as an add-on for commercial projects where in-house editing is preferred.
